title: Dar Ibn Luqman
description: Historical house built in 975 AD, famous for being the imprisonment site of King Louis IX of France during the Seventh Crusade in 1250 AD.

title: Shinnawi Palace
description: A magnificent Italian-style palace built by Mohamed Bek El-Shinnawi in 1928, considered one of the most beautiful architectural gems in Mansoura.

title: Al-Muwafi Mosque
description: One of the most prominent historic mosques in Mansoura, founded by Sheikh Abdullah Al-Muwafi about 1738 AD.

title: Al-Salhi Mosque
description: An ancient mosque in Mansoura renovated during the Ottoman era, known for its distinct minaret and spiritual significance.

title: Al-Abbasi Mosque
description: Built in 1899 during the reign of Khedive Abbas Hilmi II, it remains a central religious landmark in Mansoura.

title: Al-Sargani Mosque
description: A historic mosque located in the heart of Mansoura, dating back to the late Ottoman period.

title: The Khedivial Palace
description: Originally built as a rest house for Khedive Ismail, it now serves as a judicial building (Mixed Court previously), showcasing 19th-century architecture.

title: Umm Kulthum Square
description: A major square honoring the 'Star of the East', Umm Kulthum, featuring her statue, symbolizing her roots in the governorate.

title: Tell El-Rub (Mendes)
description: Ruins of the ancient city of Mendes, capital of the 16th nome. It flourished during the 26th Dynasty and contains the sanctuary of the Ram God.

title: Naos of Nectanebo II
description: A massive monolithic shrine (Naos) made of green granite dedicated to the god Shu, located within the temple enclosure of Mendes.

title: Tell Timai (Thmuis)
description: Located south of Mendes, Thmuis succeeded it as the capital in the Greco-Roman period. Excavations reveal extensive Hellenistic ruins.

title: Tell El-Moqdam (Leontopolis)
description: The site of the ancient city of the Lion God (Maahes). It contains remains of a temple and royal tombs from the 23rd Dynasty.

title: Tomb of Queen Kama
description: Discovered at Tell El-Moqdam, belonging to Queen Kama, grandmother of King Osorkon IV, containing significant funerary jewelry.

title: Tell El-Balamun
description: Ancient Diospolis Inferior, the northernmost city of Pharaonic Egypt. Contains temples of Amun-Ra and huge enclosure walls.

title: Tell Tebilla
description: Known as Ro-Nefer in ancient times. Excavations have yielded artifacts like statues and amulets from the Late Period.

title: Tell El-Qanater
description: An archaeological mound containing remains of settlement layers from the Greco-Roman period.

title: Tell El-Ghardqa
description: An archaeological site near the coast, containing remains of domestic structures and pottery workshops.

title: Tell El-Farkha
description: Important site for understanding the unification of Egypt, containing breweries and administrative buildings from the Naqada culture.

title: El-Ghamri Mosque
description: Historic mosque named after the city of Mit Ghamr, featuring classic Mamluk architectural style.

title: Fatima Al-Nabawiya Mosque
description: A revered mosque in Mit Ghamr dedicated to Sayyida Fatima Al-Nabawiya, a place of local pilgrimage and reflection.

title: Sheikh Shaaban Mosque
description: One of the older mosques in Mit Ghamr, known for its spiritual atmosphere and traditional design.

title: Church of Mar Girgis (Mit Ghamr)
description: An old Coptic Orthodox church dedicated to St. George, serving the large Christian community in Mit Ghamr.

title: Church of the Virgin Mary
description: Ancient church in Dakadous, known for its historic icons and as a stop on the local Holy Family traditions.

title: Tomb of Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr
description: Shrine attributed to Muhammad ibn Abi Bakr, the son of the first Caliph, located in the village of Mit Damsis.

title: St. George Church (Mit Damsis)
description: Famous for the annual festival of St. George, this church is a major pilgrimage site for Copts in the Delta.

title: Monastery of Saint Demiana
description: One of the oldest and most important monasteries in Egypt, established by Queen Demiana and her 40 virgins. It is a major pilgrimage center.

title: Old Church of St. Demiana
description: The ancient church located within the monastery walls, containing the tomb of the saint.

title: Tunnies Island (Tennis)
description: Ruins of the medieval city of Tennis on an island in Lake Manzala, once famous for its textiles (tiraz) before being abandoned.

title: Ibn Salam Mosque
description: Located on an island in the lake, comprising the tomb and mosque of the ascetic Sheikh Ibn Salam.

title: Umm Kulthum Museum
description: A cultural center and museum in the village where the legendary singer Umm Kulthum was born, displaying her personal belongings.

title: Tell El-Lali
description: Archaeological mound near Lake Manzala with remains of settlements from the late Roman period.

title: Tell El-Samara
description: One of the archaeological hills surrounding the Mendes area, contributing to the understanding of the region's history.

title: Mosque of Sidi Ali El-Gomhouri
description: A historic mosque located in the city of Manzala, featuring Ottoman architectural elements.

title: Mansoura National Museum
description: Located in Dar Ibn Luqman (often associated), it exhibits artifacts from the battle against Louis IX and local history.

title: Zawiyat Al-Amir Hammad
description: A small historic mosque or zawiya dating back to the Mamluk era in Mit Ghamr.

title: Tell El-Belamun Temple Enclosure
description: The massive mud-brick enclosure walls of the temple precinct at Tell El-Balamun, visible from the surrounding fields.
